summary: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp when deploying ASP.NET Core WebAPI to Azure Container Apps via .NET Aspire, despite referencing SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies. The error indicates libfontconfig.so.1 is missing, which means the fontconfig-dependent binary is being loaded instead of the NoDependencies variant.

bugSignals.errorMessage: System.DllNotFoundException: Unable to load shared library 'libSkiaSharp' or one of its dependencies. libfontconfig.so.1: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
bugSignals.stackTrace: System.TypeInitializationException → System.DllNotFoundException at SkiaSharp.SkiaApi.sk_managedstream_set_procs → SKAbstractManagedStream..cctor() → SKManagedStream..ctor() → SKCodec.WrapManagedStream() → SKBitmap.Decode()

stepsToReproduce: Create ASP.NET Core WebAPI project with SkiaSharp and SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies
Deploy via .NET Aspire to Azure Container Apps (Linux containers)
Call an endpoint that uses SKBitmap.Decode()
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Azure Container Apps, Linux containers, .NET 9.0.10, deployed via .NET Aspire

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The NoDependencies packaging structure has not changed fundamentally since 3.116.0. The issue is likely with how .NET Aspire deploys native assets, not a SkiaSharp code change.

regression.reason: Reporter claims 2.88.9 worked but the deployment stack changed (.NET Aspire). The fontconfig error indicates the wrong binary variant is being deployed — this is a deployment configuration issue, not a SkiaSharp regression. The NoDependencies variant would not reference libfontconfig.so.1 at all.

analysis.summary: The root cause is a native binary mismatch: the reporter references SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies but the deployed container loads the fontconfig-dependent variant (from SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux or the auto-included Win32/macOS native assets conflicting at RID resolution). The NoDependencies binary has zero third-party dependencies (only libc/libm/libpthread/libdl) and would never produce a libfontconfig.so.1 error. .NET Aspire container deployment may resolve a different NativeAssets variant or the NoDependencies package may not be referenced in the correct (executable) project.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the reporter has a clear DllNotFoundException with stack trace in a deployment scenario. Area is libSkiaSharp.native because this is about native binary loading/deployment, not the managed API. The fontconfig dependency error from NoDependencies is a known diagnostic pattern documented in packages.md — it means the wrong binary was deployed. This is not a SkiaSharp code regression but a deployment/packaging interaction with .NET Aspire.

keySignals.text: libfontconfig.so.1: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
keySignals.interpretation: The NoDependencies variant never links to fontconfig. This error proves the fontconfig-dependent binary was loaded instead, meaning NoDependencies was not deployed correctly.
Reporter correctly references NoDependencies, but the deployed binary doesn't match — deployment tooling is substituting or overriding the native asset.
Reporter's workaround confirms the issue: switching to the fontconfig-dependent variant and installing fontconfig in a custom Docker image resolves the loading error.
Secondary issue — .NET trimming strips transitive dependencies in container deployment. Not a SkiaSharp bug but affects the same deployment scenario.

codeInvestigation.finding: NoDependencies package ships from output/native/linuxnodeps/ and output/native/alpinenodeps/ paths with runtimes/linux-{arch}/native/ layout. The package explicitly excludes fontconfig — confirmed by the csproj PackageNotes listing only libpthread, libdl, libm, libc as dependencies.

The full NativeAssets.Linux package ships from output/native/linux/ paths to the same runtimes/linux-{arch}/native/ layout. Both packages target the same RID paths, so NuGet/RID resolution could pick either binary if both are transitively referenced.

SkiaSharp auto-includes NativeAssets for Win32 and macOS when TargetFramework has no platform suffix (net9.0), but does NOT auto-include NativeAssets.Linux. Linux native assets must always be manually added. This is correct behavior — the reporter did add NoDependencies manually.

workarounds: Switch from SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies to SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux and use a custom Docker base image with fontconfig installed (as reporter did with ghcr.io/avantipoint/aspnet-skia images)
Add ExcludeAssets="native" to the SkiaSharp PackageReference to prevent auto-included Win32/macOS native assets from interfering, then explicitly add NoDependencies
Verify the published output contains the correct libSkiaSharp.so by running ldd on it — NoDependencies variant should show only libc/libm/libpthread/libdl
nextQuestions: Is .NET Aspire container publishing using a custom SDK container build that may resolve different RIDs than expected?
Does the NoDependencies package appear in the final container's runtimes/linux-x64/native/ directory, or is it being overridden?
Are there transitive NuGet references pulling in SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux alongside NoDependencies?

resolution.hypothesis: .NET Aspire container deployment is not deploying the NoDependencies native binary, likely due to RID resolution conflicts, transitive package references, or the Aspire SDK container build process overriding native asset selection.

proposals.title: Use NativeAssets.Linux with custom Docker image
proposals.description: Switch to SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux and create a custom Docker base image that includes fontconfig and other native dependencies. The reporter's workaround at ghcr.io/avantipoint/aspnet-skia:9.0 already implements this pattern.

proposals.title: Verify NoDependencies deployment with ldd
proposals.description: Diagnose whether the correct binary is being deployed by inspecting the container's published output. Run ldd on the deployed libSkiaSharp.so to confirm which variant was included. If it shows fontconfig dependency, the NoDependencies package was not used.

proposals.title: Exclude transitive native assets
proposals.description: Add ExcludeAssets="native" to the SkiaSharp PackageReference to prevent any auto-included native assets from conflicting, then explicitly reference only SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies in the executable project.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use NativeAssets.Linux with custom Docker image
resolution.recommendedReason: Reporter already validated this approach works. It's the most reliable path for container deployments that need font operations, and the custom base image pattern is reusable across projects.

actionability.reason: The reporter self-resolved with a workaround and posted the solution. The root cause is a deployment configuration issue (wrong native binary deployed), not a SkiaSharp code bug. The troubleshooting guidance in packages.md covers this exact scenario.

actions.description: Labels are already correct — type/bug, area/libSkiaSharp.native, os/Linux, tenet/compatibility
Acknowledge the report, confirm the diagnosis, and thank the reporter for sharing their workaround
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed error output and for sharing your workaround — that's really helpful for others running into this.

The key diagnostic here is the `libfontconfig.so.1` error. The `NoDependencies` variant of `libSkiaSharp.so` has zero third-party dependencies (only libc/libm/libpthread/libdl), so it would never produce a fontconfig error. This means the deployed container was loading the fontconfig-dependent binary instead of the NoDependencies one — likely due to how .NET Aspire's container publishing resolves native assets.

Your approach of switching to `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux` with a custom base image that includes the required native libraries is the right fix for this deployment scenario. For anyone else hitting this:

1. Use `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux` (not NoDependencies) if your container image includes fontconfig
2. Or verify NoDependencies is actually deployed by running `ldd` on the `libSkiaSharp.so` in your published output
3. Ensure the NativeAssets package is referenced in the **executable** project, not just a library

If anyone can reproduce the NoDependencies package being ignored during Aspire deployment, we'd appreciate a minimal repro — that would help determine whether this is an Aspire SDK container publishing issue.

actions.description: Link to similar DllNotFoundException issue on Linux
